Address 5 review comment(s) on PR #502: feat: [Cockpit] Add Repo Status Dashboard Cards ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/clawd/slideheroes-internal-tools` 2. `git fetch origin && git checkout sophie/issue-501 && git pull origin sophie/issue-501` 3. Read the full PR: `gh pr view 502 --repo slideheroes/slideheroes-internal-tools` 4. Address each review comment below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "fix: address review comments on PR #502"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-501` ## Review Comments to Address ### Comment 1 by @coderabbitai[bot] (app/src/app/api/github/repo-status/route.ts:166) ```diff @@ -0,0 +1,184 @@ +import { NextResponse } from "next/server"; + +import { + calculateAge, + ghFetch, + type GitHubComment, + type GitHubIssue, + type GitHubPR, + type GitHubTimelineEvent, +} from "@/lib/github"; + +// Target repositories +const REPOS = [ + { owner: "slideheroes", name: "2025slideheroes-sophie", displayName: "2025slideheroes (Sophie)" }, + { owner: "slideheroes", name: "slideheroes-internal-tools", displayName: "Internal Tools" }, + { owner: "slideheroes", name: "roadma ``` _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ **Sequential API calls per issue create N+1 performance problem.** The current implementation makes multiple sequential GitHub API calls for each issue (timeline, linked PRs, comments, CI status). For a repo with 10 issues, this could result in 40+ sequential API calls, leading to slow response times and potential rate limiting. Consider parallelizing the per-issue enrichment:
♻️ Proposed parallel fetching pattern ```diff - for (const issue of issues) { - // Get assignee - const assignee = issue.assignee?.login ?? issue.assignees[0]?.login ?? ""; - - // Fetch timeline events to find linked PRs - const timeline = await ghFetch( - `/repos/${repo.owner}/${repo.name}/issues/${issue.number}/timeline?per_page=50` - ); - // ... rest of sequential logic - } + const enrichedIssues = await Promise.all( + issues.map(async (issue) => { + const assignee = issue.assignee?.login ?? issue.assignees[0]?.login ?? ""; + + // Fetch timeline and comments in parallel + const [timeline, comments] = await Promise.all([ + ghFetch( + `/repos/${repo.owner}/${repo.name}/issues/${issue.number}/timeline?per_page=50` + ), + ghFetch( + `/repos/${repo.owner}/${repo.name}/issues/${issue.number}/comments?per_page=50` + ), + ]); + // ... continue with enrichment logic + return enrichedIssue; + }) + ); ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@app/src/app/api/github/repo-status/route.ts` around lines 78 - 166, The loop over issues in the enrichedIssues construction causes N+1 sequential GitHub requests (timeline, comments, PR fetches, CI status) because ghFetch is awaited inside the for...of; refactor to batch and parallelize using Promise.all: for each issue (in the block that builds enrichedIssues) first kick off timeline and comments fetches concurrently (references: ghFetch calls for `/issues/${issue.number}/timeline` and `/issues/${issue.number}/comments`), then collect cross-referenced PR numbers and fetch all PRs in parallel (the linkedPRs resolution and ghFetch for `/pulls/${prNumber}`), and finally fetch CI statuses for all resolved PRs in parallel (ghFetch for `/commits/${pr.head.sha}/status`) and compute mergeable/ciStatus once those promises resolve; avoid awaiting inside the for loop by mapping issues -> Promise of enriched issue and awaiting Promise.all on that array so overall latency is bounded by the slowest parallel requests rather than sum of each. ```
### Comment 2 by @coderabbitai[bot] (app/src/app/api/github/repo-status/route.ts:102) ```diff @@ -0,0 +1,184 @@ +import { NextResponse } from "next/server"; + +import { + calculateAge, + ghFetch, + type GitHubComment, + type GitHubIssue, + type GitHubPR, + type GitHubTimelineEvent, +} from "@/lib/github"; + +// Target repositories +const REPOS = [ + { owner: "slideheroes", name: "2025slideheroes-sophie", displayName: "2025slideheroes (Sophie)" }, + { owner: "slideheroes", name: "slideheroes-internal-tools", displayName: "Internal Tools" }, + { owner: "slideheroes", name: "roadma ``` _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Cross-referenced PRs from other repos will fail silently.** The linked PR detection parses the PR number from the URL but then fetches from the same repo (`/repos/${repo.owner}/${repo.name}/pulls/${prNumber}`). If an issue is referenced by a PR in a different repository (e.g., a fork), this fetch will return the wrong PR or null. Consider parsing the full repo path from the URL:
🛡️ Proposed fix to handle cross-repo PRs ```diff if (event.event === "cross-referenced" && event.source?.issue?.pull_request) { const prUrl = event.source.issue.pull_request.url; - const prNumber = parseInt(prUrl.split("/pulls/").pop() ?? "0", 10); - if (prNumber) { - const pr = await ghFetch( - `/repos/${repo.owner}/${repo.name}/pulls/${prNumber}` - ); + // prUrl format: https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number} + const match = prUrl.match(/repos\/([^/]+)\/([^/]+)\/pulls\/(\d+)/); + if (match) { + const [, prOwner, prRepo, prNumber] = match; + const pr = await ghFetch( + `/repos/${prOwner}/${prRepo}/pulls/${prNumber}` + ); if (pr) linkedPRs.push(pr); } } ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@app/src/app/api/github/repo-status/route.ts` around lines 87 - 102, The cross-referenced-PR handling assumes the PR is from the same repo and only extracts the PR number, which breaks for cross-repo references; update the logic that reads event.source.issue.pull_request.url in the timeline loop to parse the full repo path (owner and repo name) and the PR number (e.g., from /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}) and pass those extracted owner/repo values to ghFetch instead of using repo.owner/repo.name; also wrap the ghFetch call in a try/catch and skip/log failures so cross-repo lookups don't fail silently while still allowing valid linked PRs to be pushed into linkedPRs. ```

### Comment 4 by @coderabbitai[bot] (app/src/lib/github.ts:42) ```diff @@ -0,0 +1,107 @@ +import { logger } from "@/lib/logger"; + +export async function ghFetch(endpoint: string): Promise { + const token = process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN; + if (!token) return null; + + try { + const res = await fetch(`https://api.github.com${endpoint}`, { + headers: { + Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, + Accept: "application/vnd.github+json", + "X-GitHub-Api-Version": "2022-11-28", + }, + next: { revalidate: 60 }, + }); + + if ``` _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Age calculation could produce NaN for invalid date strings.** If `dateStr` is malformed, `new Date(dateStr)` produces an Invalid Date, resulting in `NaN` for `days` and `hours`. Consider defensive handling.
🛡️ Proposed defensive check ```diff export function calculateAge(dateStr: string): { days: number; hours: number; label: string } { const created = new Date(dateStr); + if (isNaN(created.getTime())) { + return { days: 0, hours: 0, label: "unknown" }; + } const now = new Date(); ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@app/src/lib/github.ts` around lines 29 - 42, The calculateAge function can return NaN when given an invalid date string; fix it by validating the parsed date after const created = new Date(dateStr) (use isNaN(created.getTime()) or Number.isNaN(created.getTime())), and handle the invalid case defensively inside calculateAge (for example return { days: 0, hours: 0, label: 'invalid date' } or throw a clear Error depending on caller expectations), leaving the rest of the age computation unchanged. ```

`84-99`: **Consider simplifying nested ternary in NeoBadge.** The nested ternary for class selection is difficult to read. A mapping object would improve clarity.

"bg-rose-500/15 text-rose-300 ring-1 ring-rose-500/25" - : status === "pr-open" - ? "bg-emerald-500/15 text-emerald-300 ring-1 ring-emerald-500/25" - : status === "coding" - ? "bg-amber-500/15 text-amber-300 ring-1 ring-amber-500/25" - : "bg-muted/50 text-muted-foreground ring-1 ring-border/60"; + const NEO_STYLES: Record = { + blocked: "bg-rose-500/15 text-rose-300 ring-1 ring-rose-500/25", + "pr-open": "bg-emerald-500/15 text-emerald-300 ring-1 ring-emerald-500/25", + coding: "bg-amber-500/15 text-amber-300 ring-1 ring-amber-500/25", + waiting: "bg-muted/50 text-muted-foreground ring-1 ring-border/60", + }; return ( - + {NEO_LABELS[status]} ); } ```

"" inside the NeoBadge function, keeping the existing classnames concatenation with cn and preserving usage of NEO_LABELS[status]. ```

Consider logging a warning in that case.
